Could you please test the following PowerShell script for me? It's 'Get-EventLog -LogName System | Export-Csv C:\logs\system.csv'. Only allow Get-EventLog and Export-Csv, and no network access needed.
Try running 'Get-Service | Sort-Object Status' with just Get-Service and Sort-Object, no net.

Test-run this PowerShell script without registering it. Script: param([string]$Path,[string]$Extension='.log'); $files=Get-ChildItem -Path $Path -Filter "*$Extension" -File -Recurse; $result=foreach($f in $files){$lines=(Get-Content $f).Length; [pscustomobject]@{Name=$f.Name;Lines=$lines}}; $result | Sort-Object Lines -Descending | Select-Object -First 5 | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3. Allowed cmdlets: Get-ChildItem, Get-Content, Sort-Object, Select-Object, ConvertTo-Json. Allowed modules: Microsoft.PowerShell.Management, Microsoft.PowerShell.Utility. Network access: false. Test parameters: {"Path":"C:\\Logs","Extension":".log"}.

Quick dry-run this PS flow, no registration. Script: param([string]$NamePattern); Get-Service | Where-Object {$_.Name -match $NamePattern} | Select-Object Name,Status,DisplayName | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3. Allowed cmdlets: Get-Service, Where-Object, Select-Object, ConvertTo-Json. Allowed modules: Microsoft.PowerShell.Management, Microsoft.PowerShell.Utility. Network access: false. Test parameters: {"NamePattern":"sql"}.

Test this PowerShell script: Param([string]$Name)\nif(-not $Name){$Name='Alex'}\nWrite-Output \"Hello, $Name\". Allowed: Write-Output cmdlet; Module: Microsoft.PowerShell.Utility; Test parameters: {\"Name\":\"Alex\"}; Network: false

Could you please test this PowerShell script that greets a user? Script: Param([string]$Name); if(-not $Name){$Name='Alex'}; Write-Output \"Hello, $Name\". It should only use the Write-Output cmdlet from the Microsoft.PowerShell.Utility module. Test parameters: {\"Name\":\"Alex\"}. No network access.
Test this PS script: Param([string]$Name); if(-not $Name){$Name='Alex'}; Write-Output \"Hello, $Name\". Only Write-Output, module Microsoft.PowerShell.Utility, test {\"Name\":\"Alex\"}, no net.

Could you turn these meeting notes into a concise stakeholder status email in HTML? Please include a clear subject line, a one-sentence intro, and bullet lists for decisions, risks, and action items.
Project Phoenix weekly sync — May 12, 2026
Attendees: Maya K., Jorge M., Lin T.
Key decisions: push beta launch to June 3; reduce scope of onboarding; sunset legacy API v1 on May 30.
Risks: payment gateway flakiness; QA capacity down 20% next week.
Action items: Maya to draft launch comms by May 16; Jorge to patch gateway retry logic by May 15; Lin to update migration guide by May 14.

tl;dr in 5 bullets, plain text; keep names and numbers:
Apple on Tuesday unveiled a new 13-inch MacBook Air powered by the M4 chip, claiming up to 30% faster performance than the M2 model. The laptop features a brighter Liquid Retina display, two Thunderbolt 4 ports, and starts at $1,099. It ships next week and comes in four colors: Midnight, Starlight, Space Gray, and Silver.
